questions.q: What was the main purpose of building Rama Setu, the causeway to Lanka?
options: To let Rama's army cross the sea to reach Lanka
To trap fish for the army's food
To build a city in the middle of the ocean
To honor the sea god with a monument
questions.explanation: The bridge was raised across the sea so that Rama's vanara army could march over it to besiege Lanka and rescue Sita.

questions.q: Which army built Rama Setu?
options: An army of human soldiers from Ayodhya
questions.explanation: Rama's vast vanara (monkey) army, led under his command, constructed the bridge of stones across the ocean.

questions.q: What was the bridge primarily made of?
questions.explanation: Rama Setu was a long bridge built from stones and boulders cast into the sea by the vanaras.

questions.q: To which island kingdom did Rama Setu lead?
questions.explanation: The causeway connected the mainland to the island of Lanka, the kingdom ruled by Ravana.

questions.q: Whom did Rama seek to rescue by crossing the bridge to Lanka?
questions.explanation: Rama led his army across to Lanka to free his wife Sita, who had been abducted by Ravana.

questions.q: Who had abducted Sita and taken her to Lanka?
questions.explanation: Ravana, the demon king of Lanka, abducted Sita, prompting Rama's campaign across the sea.

questions.q: Across what body of water was Rama Setu built?
questions.explanation: The causeway was raised across the ocean separating the mainland from the island of Lanka.

questions.q: Which devoted vanara was a key figure in Rama's army during the Lanka campaign?
questions.explanation: Hanuman, the mighty and devoted vanara, played a central role in serving Rama throughout the war against Lanka.

questions.q: What kind of place is Rama Setu best described as?
questions.explanation: Rama Setu is a bridge, or causeway, of stones spanning the sea to Lanka.

questions.q: Who was the king and commander leading the army that crossed Rama Setu?
questions.explanation: Rama, the prince of Ayodhya, led his army across the bridge to wage war on Lanka.

questions.q: Which vanara is traditionally credited as the chief architect or engineer of Rama Setu?
questions.explanation: Nala, son of the divine architect Vishvakarma, directed the construction of the bridge in the Valmiki Ramayana.

questions.q: Before building the bridge, Rama performed a penance on the shore to gain passage. Whom was he appealing to?
questions.explanation: Rama undertook a penance to entreat Samudra, the lord of the ocean, to allow his army to cross.

questions.q: When the ocean deity initially did not respond, what did Rama threaten to do?
options: Drain the sea with fiery arrows
Walk across the water by yogic power
questions.explanation: Angered by the ocean's silence, Rama took up his bow and threatened to dry up the sea with his blazing weapons until Samudra appeared.

questions.q: According to the Ramayana, how many days did the vanaras take to complete the bridge?
questions.explanation: Valmiki's Ramayana states the vanaras completed the great bridge across the sea in five days.

questions.q: From which coastal region did the bridge to Lanka traditionally begin?
questions.explanation: Tradition places the bridge's starting point at the southern coast near Rameswaram, leading toward Lanka.

questions.q: What detail is often said about how the stones used in the bridge behaved in the water?
questions.explanation: In popular tradition the stones floated on the water, an aspect linked in later lore to Rama's name being inscribed on them.

questions.q: Which two brothers from Ravana's lineage joined Rama before the crossing, one of them having defected?
options: Vibhishana defected to Rama, while Ravana and Kumbhakarna opposed him
Kumbhakarna defected to Rama against Ravana
Indrajit defected to Rama against Ravana
Mareecha defected to Rama against Ravana
questions.explanation: Vibhishana, Ravana's brother, abandoned Lanka and sought refuge with Rama, while Ravana and Kumbhakarna remained his foes.

questions.q: What is another common name by which Rama Setu is known?
questions.explanation: The causeway is also called Setubandha, and in English geography it is known as Adam's Bridge.

questions.q: Which large bear-like vanara leader counseled Rama and aided the war effort against Lanka?
questions.explanation: Jambavan, the wise leader of the bears, was a key counselor among Rama's forces during the Lanka campaign.

questions.q: After the ocean deity appeared, what guidance did he give Rama about crossing?
options: To wait for the tide to recede
That a bridge could be built and he would bear it upon his waters
To find a hidden underwater tunnel
To summon Garuda to fly the army over
questions.explanation: Samudra advised that a bridge be constructed, promising his waters would support it so the army could cross.

questions.q: Which monkey kingdom supplied the bulk of the army that built and crossed the bridge?
questions.explanation: The vanara forces came from Kishkindha, the monkey kingdom ruled by Sugriva, Rama's ally.

questions.q: What did the vanaras chiefly do to gather and transport materials for the bridge?
options: Uprooted trees, mountains, and hurled great rocks into the sea
Wove enormous nets from jungle vines
Melted ore to cast metal beams
questions.explanation: The vanaras tore up trees and mountains and cast huge boulders and rocks into the sea to form the causeway.

questions.q: According to Valmiki's Ramayana, what was the stated length of the bridge in yojanas?
questions.explanation: Valmiki describes the bridge as one hundred yojanas in length and ten yojanas in width across the sea.

questions.q: According to Valmiki's Ramayana, what was the stated width of the bridge in yojanas?
questions.explanation: Valmiki gives the bridge's breadth as ten yojanas, against a length of one hundred yojanas.

questions.q: Nala, the bridge's architect, was said to be the son of which divine craftsman?
questions.explanation: Nala was the son of Vishvakarma, the celestial architect, and inherited his skill in construction.

questions.q: In which kanda (book) of Valmiki's Ramayana does the building of the bridge primarily occur?
questions.explanation: The construction of the bridge and the march to Lanka are narrated in the Yuddha Kanda, the book of war.

questions.q: By what name is the ocean deity whom Rama propitiated before the bridge was built most commonly known?
questions.explanation: The personified ocean Rama propitiated is called Sagara or Samudra, the lord of the seas.

questions.q: When Samudra appeared, he suggested Rama redirect his already-drawn fiery arrow toward a region troubled by robbers. This act is traditionally linked to the formation of which feature?
options: The Drumakulya desert region in the north
questions.explanation: At Samudra's request Rama loosed the arrow toward the Drumakulya region, an episode tied in the epic to that northern desert tract.

questions.q: Which two vanaras are named in tradition as supervising the placing of stones, with one often paired alongside Nala as a builder?
questions.explanation: Nala, the chief architect, is frequently paired with Nila in traditional accounts of overseeing the bridge's construction.

questions.q: What did Rama do at the seashore that is commemorated by the temple at Rameswaram, associated with the bridge's starting point?
options: He installed and worshipped a lingam of Shiva
questions.explanation: Tradition holds that Rama worshipped a Shiva lingam at Rameswaram near the bridge's starting point, commemorated by the temple there.
